headline: The FBI built a small town to simulate cyberattacks
summary: The FBI opened a 22,000 square-foot Cyber Range in Alabama. It's a replica town with a convenience store, hospital, and houses. Used for simulating and training against modern digital cyberattacks.

headline: China may have accessed Mythos
summary: The White House imposed export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos AI. Decision driven by fears of Chinese government access to Mythos. Potential access poses a serious national security risk.

headline: Conclave is the sound of a NYC summer block party
summary: The 'Conclave' album evokes the vibrant atmosphere of a NYC summer block party. The reviewer describes a personal connection to the album's mood. It offers a positive escape from personal struggles and summer heat.

headline: How to watch most of the World Cup matches with free trials
summary: This guide details how to stream World Cup matches for free. It suggests using various free trial offers from streaming services. Tips are included on combining offers to cover the entire tournament.

headline: Timothée Chalamet Takes Shot at the Oscars After Knicks Win NBA Championship
summary: Timothée Chalamet went viral celebrating the Knicks' NBA Championship win. The actor took a public jab at the Academy Awards during his celebration. The clip quickly gained traction across social media platforms.

headline: Super Mario Bros. Copy Sells for Record-Breaking $3 Million at Auction
summary: A copy of Super Mario Bros. just sold for a record-breaking $3 million. This makes it the most expensive video game ever sold at auction. The sale set a new benchmark in video game collectibles.

headline: Rockstar Co-Founder Dan Houser Says There’s No Right Way to Play an Open-World Game
summary: Rockstar co-founder Dan Houser discussed open-world games at the Tribeca Festival. He stated there's no single 'right' way to play these games. Houser emphasized player freedom and diverse approaches to gameplay.

headline: Microsoft CEO Says 'There's More Monetization of Xbox Games Happening on YouTube Than at Microsoft'
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella commented on Xbox game monetization. He believes YouTube monetizes Xbox content more than Microsoft itself. This follows Xbox boss Asha Sharma's commitment to 'reset' the gaming branch.

headline: Israeli military says Hezbollah targets struck in Beirut
summary: Israel announced strikes against Iran-backed Hezbollah targets in Beirut. Iran warned the strikes could jeopardize a US-Iran deal. The deal aims to end the ongoing fighting in the region.

headline: Assisted dying returns to parliament as MP urges peers to 'finish the job'
summary: Assisted dying legislation is being reintroduced in UK Parliament. MP Lauren Edwards urges peers to pass the previously stalled bill. The bill narrowly passed the Commons but ran out of time in the Lords.

headline: Royal Marines board Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel
summary: Royal Marines and National Crime Agency officers boarded a Russian oil tanker. The six-hour operation occurred in the English Channel early Sunday. The tanker belongs to Russia's 'shadow fleet'.

headline: UK electric car sales target set to be weakened
summary: The UK's target for electric car sales is expected to be lowered. Different potential new targets are currently under consideration. Details of the revised target have not yet been finalized.
